issues.tradition_claim: The verse is Krishna's first words to Arjuna: a sharp diagnostic correction naming the collapse as kaśmala (impurity / faintheartedness), as anārya (unworthy of the noble), and as serving none of Arjuna's stated ends (neither higher worlds nor reputation).
issues.engineering_claim: The verse is the senior incident commander's first sharp correction of a frozen senior on-call: 'where is this collapse coming from, right now? Run the rollback playbook step 4.'
issues.explanation: The verse is re-scoped from a battlefield correction to an incident-channel correction. This is acceptable translation across domains — the underlying claim (sharp diagnostic correction at the critical hour, addressed senior-to-senior, naming the collapse as inconsistent with who the operator is) is preserved. The asvargyam (higher-worlds) clause is the only piece that drops, which is a scope choice. No inversion, no contradiction, no weakening. Distortion-test passes.
